CHANGELOG.DontShowAgain: Don't show again for this version

ACTIONS.ImportShortChallengeVignetteJSON: Import Short Challenge / Vignette from JSON

THEMEKITS.NoThemekitsAvailable: No themekits available. Create themekit items first.

ThemekitSourcePacks.MenuHint: Choose which compendiums — and whether items in the world — may supply themekits in the Themekit selector.
ThemekitSourcePacks.Explanation: Check the compendiums that should provide themekits in the selector. Leaving none checked — or checking all of them — allows every compendium, including any installed later (default behavior). The world-items checkbox above is independent: it simply toggles whether items in this world are offered.

QUESTIONS.ConfirmDeletion: Are you sure you want to delete this?
QUESTIONS.DeleteItem: Are you sure you want to delete this item?
QUESTIONS.DeleteChallenge: Are you sure you want to delete this Challenge?
QUESTIONS.RemoveFellowshipThemecardConfirmation: Are you sure you want to remove the Fellowship Themecard assignment? This will not delete the Themecard Actor, just unassign it from this character.
QUESTIONS.RemoveCustomBackgroundConfirmation: Are you sure you want to remove the custom background? This will not delete the image, just remove it from this character.
QUESTIONS.RemoveThemekitConfirmation: Are you sure you want to remove this themekit? This will not delete the themekit item, just unassign it from this character's themebook.

NOTIFICATIONS.NotAllowedToUploadFilesForCustomBackgrou: You do not have permission to upload files. Please contact your GM.
NOTIFICATIONS.NoFellowshipThemecardFound: No Fellowship Themecard actors found. Use "Create New" to make one first.

HELP.BackpackSheet: Backpack entries are edited directly on the character sheet (edit mode of the Backpack card). Use the Description tab for tooltip text.
HELP.QuintessenceSheet: Edit this quintessence's text in the Description tab — it is shown as a tooltip on the character sheet.
HELP.FellowshipCreation: No Fellowship Themecard is assigned. Create a new one, or assign an existing Fellowship Themecard Actor.
HELP.NoThemebooksInCharacter: No Themebooks found. Create Themebook Items and drop them here.
HELP.ChallengeTemplateDragNDrop: Drag and drop challenge / vignette items on this NPC to use them. They serve as templates and will not modify the original item.
HELP.FelllowshipThemeCardExplanation: A Fellowship Themecard is a shared resource for all members of a fellowship. It contains power tags, weakness tags and special improvements. The actual tags are getting entered in the characters. Not here, here are only the questions of the tags.
HELP.GMTagShortCuts: Select text, then CTL+SHIFT+S = Status, CTL+SHIFT+X = Might, CTL+SHIFT+A = Tag, CTL+SHIFT+Y = Bold
HELP.ChallengeAddon: Define a reusable set of roles, tags/statuses, limits, threats, secrets and special features. Drag this add-on onto a Challenge to merge it in and raise the Challenge's rating.
HELP.MightyAspects: Aspects in which this Challenge is Adventure or Greatness level (everything else is Origin). These are NOT tags — they tell the GM whether a Might differential (Favored/Imperiled) applies; they do not add to a roll.

Titles.RightClickToToggleBurnState: Right click to toggle burn state
Titles.PlannedPowertag: Planned Power Or Weakness Tag. Select it to hide it from the active tags list until you are ready to use it.
Titles.PlannedStorytag: Planned Story Tag. Select it to hide it from the active story tags list until you are ready to use it.

SCENE_APP.NoActiveScene: The scene tracker needs an active scene. Activate a scene first.
SCENE_APP.WeaknessToggleHint: Click to toggle this weakness as active in the scene
SCENE_APP.NoJourneyAssigned: No journey assigned. Drag a journey from the sidebar onto this app to assign it to the scene.
SCENE_APP.OnlyStoryThemes: Only themebooks flagged as a Story Theme can be added here.
SCENE_APP.NoStoryThemes: No story themes yet. Drag a Story Theme from the sidebar or a compendium onto this app to add it to the scene.

CHALLENGE_ADDON.OnlyOnChallenges: Challenge Add-ons can only be dropped onto a Challenge (NPC).
CHALLENGE_ADDON.ConfirmApply: Apply the add-on "{name}" to {challenge}? This will merge in its roles, tags, statuses, limits, threats, secrets and special features, and raise the Challenge's rating.
CHALLENGE_ADDON.ConfirmReapply: "{name}" has already been applied to {challenge}. Apply it again anyway? This will merge it in a second time.

ROTE.PractitionersPlaceholder: Who may have access to this rote?
ROTE.SelectHint: Click to select this rote as a tag for the next roll

COLLAB.HelpRequested: Help requested from your fellow heroes…
COLLAB.HelpPrompt: {actor} is taking an action — contribute one of your tags (+1 Power)?
COLLAB.GroupHint: A single roll for the group. Each hero contributes one tag (+1 Power); one hero may burn a tag (+3). The outcome affects everyone.
COLLAB.GroupStarted: Group action started — heroes may contribute a tag.
COLLAB.GroupPrompt: Contribute one tag to the group action.
COLLAB.BurnForPower: Burn this tag for +3 Power (only one hero may burn)
COLLAB.AffectsGroup: The outcome affects the entire group.

TUTORIAL.Title: Mist Engine — Narrator Tour
TUTORIAL.Description: A short guided tour of the GM tools this system adds to Foundry.
TUTORIAL.WelcomeContent: This tour shows the tools the Mist Engine system adds for you. Most of them live here in the scene controls on the left. Use the arrow keys or the buttons to move through the tour.
TUTORIAL.ToolsContent: Under the Notes controls you'll find the system's GM apps: the Scene Tracker, Camping & Sojourns, Acting Together and How To Play. We'll look at each one.
TUTORIAL.SceneTrackerContent: Opens the scene's shared story tags, statuses and dice-roll modifiers, plus the characters and challenges present. Tags you add here flow automatically into the players' rolls. It needs an active scene.
TUTORIAL.CampingContent: Runs a camping or sojourn scene (Core Book p. 179): campsite tags, Rest / Reflect / Camp Action for each hero, and the backpack story-tag expiration review. Also needs an active scene.
TUTORIAL.GroupActionContent: Opens a shared group action (Core Book p. 131): one roll for the whole group. Each hero contributes one tag (+1 Power) and a single hero may burn a tag (+3); you can add a Narrator modifier. The outcome affects everyone.
TUTORIAL.CharSheetContent: This is a hero's sheet — themes and their power tags, the backpack, quintessences, the fellowship and rotes. In Game Mode, players click a power tag to select it, then roll from the buttons at the top.
TUTORIAL.EditToggleContent: This lock button in the title bar toggles the sheet between Game Mode (locked — for playing and rolling) and Edit Mode (open — for building the character). Players use Edit Mode to fill in themes, tags, quests and the backpack.
TUTORIAL.EditModeContent: In Edit Mode the cards become editable: add themebooks, power and weakness tags, quests, special improvements and backpack items. Cards can be dragged to reorder them or moved to the Other tab.
TUTORIAL.GameModeContent: Back in Game Mode the roll buttons drive play: Quick, Detailed, Reaction and Sacrifice. The player selects the relevant tags and statuses, then rolls — the dialog totals the Power for them.
TUTORIAL.ChallengesContent: Challenge (NPC) sheets hold limits, mighty aspects and threats. When a status reaches a limit's tier the challenge shows an 'Overcome' badge; heroes are overcome by a tier-5 status. Statuses stack on their tracking card automatically.
TUTORIAL.RollConfirmContent: In the system settings you can enable 'GM Roll Confirmation': player rolls are then sent to you for approval first, showing their full tag selection before the dice fall.
TUTORIAL.HowToPlayContent: The How To Play window is your in-app rules reference. That's the tour — you can replay it anytime from Settings → Tour Management.

PLAYER_TUTORIAL.Title: Mist Engine — Hero Tour
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.Description: A short guided tour of your character sheet and how to play as a hero.
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.WelcomeContent: This is your character sheet — your hero's portrait, name and a short description at the top. Everything you need to play lives on this sheet. Use the arrow keys or the buttons to move through the tour.
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.EditToggleContent: This lock button in the title bar switches the sheet between Game Mode (locked — for playing and rolling) and Edit Mode (open — for building your hero). You'll spend most of play in Game Mode.
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.EditModeContent: In Edit Mode the cards open up: add themebooks with their power and weakness tags, quests, special improvements, quintessences and backpack items. Cards can be dragged to reorder them or moved to the Other tab.
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.ThemesContent: Your themes are the heart of your hero. Each holds power tags (your strengths) and weakness tags. In Game Mode you click a power tag to select it for the next roll — a circle marks the chosen tags. Rotes, if you have any, appear here as their own cards too.
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.RollingContent: These buttons drive play: Quick, Detailed and Reaction. Select the tags and statuses that apply, then roll — the dialog totals your Power automatically. You can also drag scene tags the Narrator shares into your roll.
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.DiceDialogContent: When you make a roll, this dialog opens. It lists the power tags and statuses you selected, folds in any scene tags, and totals your Power at a glance — then two dice are rolled and that Power is added. If another player is logged in, a 'Request Help' button also appears here: a fellow hero can contribute one of their tags to boost your roll (+1 Power each).
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.SacrificeContent: A Sacrifice is a Quick outcome taken without any Power: you accept severe consequences to achieve something extraordinary. Use it when the moment calls for it — the Narrator may grant a modifier first.
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.StatusesContent: Your floating story tags and statuses collect here — helpful ones you've created and harmful ones inflicted on you. They stack automatically and can be selected for rolls just like power tags.
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.BackpackContent: Your backpack holds story tags for gear and useful items. Add an item, then select it in a roll to bring it to bear. Some items expire during Camping — the Narrator will let you know.
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.QuintessencesContent: Quintessences are the small, defining details of your hero — quirks, keepsakes and touches of flavour. Add your own here to make the character your own.
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.FellowshipContent: The fellowship card tracks your bonds with the other heroes: relationship tags and companions. These help you support one another and can be recovered during Camping.
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.OtherTabContent: Cards you don't need front-and-centre can be moved to the Other tab to keep your main view tidy — extra themes, quintessences or backpack, whatever you like.
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.BioNotesContent: The Biography and Notes tabs are yours for the story — your hero's background, and private notes only you keep. Nothing here affects rolls; it's pure roleplay.
PLAYER_TUTORIAL.HowToPlayContent: Need the rules? The How To Play window is your in-app reference — open it any time. That's the tour — you can replay it whenever you like from Settings → Tour Management.

OVERCOME.LimitReachedHint: A status has reached this Limit — the Challenge is overcome.
OVERCOME.HeroHint: A tier-5 status has overcome this Hero — they cannot act and lose control of the scene.

SACRIFICE.Title: Sacrifice
SACRIFICE.Hint: Making a Sacrifice is a Quick outcome without any Power: you accept severe Consequences to achieve something extraordinary. The Narrator may grant a modifier before you roll.
SACRIFICE.MiracleText: You succeed and your Sacrifice is lessened by one level.
SACRIFICE.FateText: You succeed but pay in full the Consequences of your Sacrifice.
SACRIFICE.InVainText: You pay in full the Consequences of your Sacrifice, but nothing comes out of it — or things go terribly wrong.

CAMPING.Title: Camping & Sojourns
CAMPING.NoActiveScene: Camping needs an active scene. Activate a scene first.
CAMPING.DurationHint: A sojourn grants bonus Power on camp actions: days +1, weeks +2, months +3.
CAMPING.Inactive: No camping or sojourn is currently in progress.
CAMPING.ThirdPeriodWarning: The third period comes at the cost of Consequences!
CAMPING.ApplyExpirationHint: All marked story tags expire and no longer grant Power.
CAMPING.BackpackReviewHintGm: Click entries to mark them for expiration. Click an expired entry to recreate it.
CAMPING.BackpackReviewHintPlayer: Propose story tags of your backpack for expiration — the Narrator has the final say.
CAMPING.RestHint: Recover and expire statuses (once per camping scene). During a sojourn even graver injuries can be expired.
CAMPING.RestAlreadyUsed: You have already rested during this camping scene.
CAMPING.ExpireStatusHint: Click to expire this status after your rest
CAMPING.ReflectHint: Internalize what you have been through: mark Improve on one of your themes (once per camping scene).
CAMPING.ReflectAlreadyUsed: You have already reflected during this camping scene.
CAMPING.CampActionHint: Take up a camp duty: select your tags as usual, then roll — or spend half your Power (rounded up) without rolling.
CAMPING.CampActionSojournHint: Sojourn bonus Power is added automatically.
CAMPING.ActionSpendHint: Requires at least 1 Power from selected tags.
CAMPING.NoPowerSelected: Select at least one tag (1 Power or more) for your camp action first.
CAMPING.FellowshipHint: Recover a scratched relationship tag or create a new one with another Hero (once per camping scene).
CAMPING.FellowshipAlreadyUsed: You have already used your Fellowship quality time.
CAMPING.ConfirmRest: Take a rest now? This can only be chosen once per camping scene and cannot be undone.
CAMPING.ConfirmReflect: Reflect and mark Improve on "{theme}"? This can only be chosen once per camping scene and cannot be undone.
CAMPING.ConfirmExpireStatus: Expire the status "{name}"? It will be removed and cannot be restored.
CAMPING.ConfirmSpendPower: Spend {spend} Power (of {power}) without rolling? Your selected tags will be consumed.
CAMPING.ConfirmRecoverFellowship: Recover the relationship tag "{name}"? This uses your Fellowship quality time for this camping scene.
CAMPING.RecoverFellowshipHint: Click to recover this scratched relationship tag
CAMPING.ChatStartedLine: {mode} begins ({duration}). Review your backpacks and choose your activities.
CAMPING.ChatEndedLine: The camping time is over — the journey continues.
CAMPING.ChatRestLine: takes time to recover — the Narrator expires appropriate statuses.
CAMPING.ChatRestLineSojourn: takes a long rest — even graver injuries may be expired.
CAMPING.ChatExpiredLine: "{name}" has expired and no longer grants Power.
CAMPING.ChatFellowshipNewLine: creates the new relationship tag "{name}" with {companion}.

GM_CONFIRM.Title: Roll Confirmation
GM_CONFIRM.Rejected: The GM rejected your roll. Adjust your selection and try again.
GM_CONFIRM.CanceledByPlayer: The player withdrew the roll request.

LABELS.DragCardHint: Drag to reorder or move between tabs
LABELS.Name: Name
LABELS.NoCharactersInScene: No characters found. Place character actors on the current scene to see them here.
LABELS.NoChallengesInScene: No challenges found. Place challenge actors on the current scene to see them here.

CustomBackgroundEditor.HelpText: The Custom Background Editor allows you to create a custom background for your character by uploading (or copy and paste) an image and adjusting its position and scale. You can also export the customized background as a PNG file or set it directly as your character's background. Please note that you need permission to upload files to use this feature. Contact your GM if you don't have the necessary permissions. The line below is just a guide to help you position your artwork, it will not be visible in the exported image or the character background.

quick.fail: The Narrator decides on a narrative development detrimental to the Hero, and can give or remove a tag or status in a way that hinders the Hero.
quick.neutral:
The action unfolds as expected, or better, achieving its intended goal or overcoming an obstacle. The Narrator can also give you a useful tag or status.

The Narrator decides on a narrative development detrimental to the Hero, and can give or remove a tag or status in a way that hinders the Hero.
quick.success: The action unfolds as expected, or better, achieving its intended goal or overcoming an obstacle. The Narrator can also give you a useful tag or status.

detailed.fail: The Narrator decides on a narrative development detrimental to the Hero, and can give or remove a tag or status in a way that hinders the Hero.
detailed.neutral:
Success (as in 10+) & Consequences as in Simple action.

The action unfolds as expected, or better, achieving its intended goal or overcoming an obstacle. The Narrator can also give you a useful tag or status.

The Narrator decides on a narrative development detrimental to the Hero, and can give or remove a tag or status in a way that hinders the Hero.

Spend your power:

  • Add or scratch a tag (2 Power)
  • Add or reduce a status (1 Power per tier)
  • Discover a valuable detail (1 Power)

See advanced options on page 154.
detailed.success:
Spend your power:
  • Add or scratch a tag (2 Power)
  • Add or reduce a status (1 Power per tier)
  • Discover a valuable detail (1 Power)

See advanced options on page 154.

No Consequences.

reaction.neutral: You succeed and may spend PWR Power only to lessen the Consequences.
reaction.success: You succeed and may spend PWR+1 Power, on any Effect.
